item.title: Drone maker AEVEX raises $320 million in US IPO
item.description: Drone maker AEVEX said on Thursday that it вҖӢhad raised $320 million in its U.S. initial public вҖҢoffering after pricing shares at $20 each.

item.title: Stocks set for weekly gain, oil below $100 on peace deal hopes
item.description: Asian stocks were poised for a second week of strong gains and вҖӢoil prices were pinned below $100 a barrel with investors hopeful for a near-term resolution to the Middle East war.

item.title: Dollar set for second weekly loss on Iran war peace hopes
item.description: The U.S. dollar was headed for a second consecutive weekly decline on Friday in tentative trade, as вҖӢa ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon and prospects for fresh Iran talks prompted investors to unwind safe-haven positions.

item.title: Philippines, US to build industrial hub to strengthen supply chain security
item.description: The United States and the Philippines will build a 4,000-acre (1,620 hectares) industrial вҖӢhub after Manila joined a Washington-led initiative to вҖҢsecure AI and semiconductor supply chains, the U.S. State Department said in a statement.

item.title: Tesla seeks Taiwan chip engineers for Terafab project
item.description: Tesla is seeking semiconductor engineers in Taiwan for its Terafab artificial intelligence chip complex, according to job вҖӢpostings on its website.

item.title: OpenAI to spend more than $20 billion on Cerebras chips, receive stake, The Information reports
item.description: OpenAI has agreed to вҖҢpay chip startup Cerebras more than $20 billion over the next three years to use servers powered by the company's chips, under a deal that could also give the ChatGPT maker an equity stake in вҖӢthe firm, The Information reported on Thursday, citing sources.

item.title: Sony to get up to $380 million image sensor factory Japan subsidy
item.description: The Japanese government will provide вҖӢsubsidies of up to вҖҢ60 billion yen ($380 million) to Sony for an вҖӢimage sensor plant вҖӢin the western Japanese prefecture вҒ of Kumamoto, industry вҖӢminister Ryosei Akazawa said вҖӢon Friday.

item.title: Oil falls on prospects for talks to end Iran war and revive supply
item.description: Oil prices fell in early trade on Friday on optimism the Middle East conflict could be nearing вҖӢan end after a 10-day ceasefire between Lebanon and Israel вҖҢtook effect and President Donald Trump said the U.S. and Iran may meet for talks on the weekend.

item.title: Panama Canal downplays report of $4 million 'line-jumping' auction payment amid higher traffic
item.description: The Panama Canal Authority on Thursday downplayed a report that an LPG vessel paid $4 million to jump the transit queue, вҖӢsaying the result reflected temporary market conditions rather than a fee вҖҢset by the waterway, as the Iran war drives more traffic to the canal.

item.title: California gasoline stocks fall to record lows as Hormuz disruption bites
item.description: CaliforniaвҖҷs gasoline inventories have declined вҖӢto record lows as fuel prices have surged nationwide on the back of the war on Iran, and analysts warn вҖҢthat the full effect of supply disruptions tied to the closure of the Strait of Hormuz has yet to hit the Golden State.

item.title: Land management company EagleRock discloses revenue rise in US IPO filing
item.description: Land management company EagleRock Land reported higher annual revenue in paperwork filed on Thursday for a U.S. вҖӢinitial public offering, positioning itself as a rare energy вҖҢlisting amid renewed investor interest in the sector.

item.title: NiSource signs long-term power deal with Alphabet, expands Amazon agreement
item.description: U.S. utility NiSource said on Thursday it had signed a long-term energy supply agreement with a unit of Alphabet to вҖӢsupport a large data center in northern Indiana.

item.title: Exclusive: Valero partially restarts Port Arthur, Texas refinery after blast, sources say
item.description: Valero Energy Corp has partially restarted its 380,000 barrel-per-day Port Arthur, Texas refinery following a March 23 explosion and fire, said two вҖӢpeople familiar with plant operations.

item.title: Trading Day: Nasdaq notches 12-day winning streak as Middle East peace talks progress
item.description: Wall Street moved higher on Thursday, as traders assessed ongoing Middle East peace negotiations and parsed a mixed bag of corporate earnings.

item.title: Fuel surge threatens Spirit Airlines bankruptcy exit, raises liquidation risk
item.description: Spirit Airlines' bankruptcy exit plan is under renewed pressure after a вҖӢsharp rise in jet fuel prices undermined key assumptions behind its restructuring.

item.title: Netflix co-founder Hastings to exit as company mulls its next move; shares fall
item.description: Netflix Chairman Reed Hastings is leaving the streaming service he co-founded 29 years ago as the company regains its footing after it lost its $72 billion deal for Warner Bros Discovery .

item.title: Exclusive: US law enforcement raids offices of Houston fuel trader Ikon Midstream
item.description: U.S. authorities have raided the offices of Ikon Midstream, a Houston fuel вҖӢtrader whose diesel exports are part of fuel-smuggling investigations in Mexico, two U.S. officials and a Mexican security official told Reuters.

item.title: Trump's energy leaders to hold call with CEOs on Iran war, source says
item.description: The heads of the U.S. Interior and Energy Departments will hold a call on Thursday вҖӢwith CEOs from energy companies including Exxon and вҖҢChevron on their potential to boost oil and gas output, an administration official said, as the Iran war hikes global вҖӢenergy prices.

item.title: OpenAI launches AI model GPT-Rosalind for life sciences research
item.description: OpenAI on Thursday introduced an artificial intelligence model touting increased biology knowledge and scientific research capabilities, вҖӢas the startup deepens its push into the вҖҢlife sciences field.

item.title: White House to give US agencies Anthropic Mythos access, Bloomberg News reports
item.description: The U.S. government is planning to make a version of Anthropic's frontier AI model Mythos available to major federal agencies amid вҖӢconcerns that the tool could sharply increase cybersecurity risk, Bloomberg вҖҢNews reported on Thursday.

item.title: Stock market bulls see signs rally could endure after S&P 500 back at highs
item.description: U.S. stocks have rebounded to record highs after the Iran war-driven selloff and there are signs under the market's surface that suggest the вҖӢrally has further to run.

item.title: Ellison takes Paramount, Warner Bros case straight to theater owners
item.description: Paramount Skydance CEO вҖӢDavid Ellison promised movie theater owners on Thursday that the company will keep its commitment to вҖҢrelease at least 30 films per year if regulators clear the proposed $110 billion purchase of Warner Bros Discovery .

item.title: Pirelli raises prices as part of Middle East crisis mitigation plan
item.description: Italian premium tyremaker Pirelli said on Thursday it had activated a "mitigation plan" to limit the вҖӢeffects of the ongoing Middle East crisis, вҖҢwhich includes price increases and additional cost cuts.

item.title: US companies that jumped ship to tech, AI businesses over the years
item.description: Sneaker company Allbirds' pivot to AI computing has revived a Wall Street trend in recent years: smaller companies leaning into tech-focused transformations to win over AI-hungry investors.

item.title: Codelco, Anglo pursue twin environmental approvals for shared Chile copper pit
item.description: Chilean copper producer Codelco (COBRE.UL) and global miner Anglo American plan to submit separate environmental studies to regulators for their planned shared copper mine in Chile, documents seen by Reuters show, using what they called an "unprecedented" twin-track to streamline the approval process.

item.title: Airflow products maker Madison Air valued at $15.7 billion as shares rise on NYSE debut
item.description: Madison Air Solutions secured a valuation of $15.65 billion after its shares jumped 18.5% in their debut on Thursday, вҖӢsignaling solid investor demand for companies tied to AI-driven data вҖҢcenter growth.

item.title: Russia-linked Grinex crypto exchange suspends operations after cyber attack
item.description: Russia-linked sanctioned crypto exchange вҖӢGrinex said on Thursday it had suspended вҖҢoperations after assets worth 1 billion roubles ($13.10 million) were stolen during a cyber attack.

item.title: USTR's Greer says offshoring, rules of origin on agenda for Mexico trade talks
item.description: U.S. businesses continue to offshore operations to Mexico despite вҖӢthe revamped regional trade pact that was meant to stymie вҖҢthat activity, U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer said on Thursday, and the product rules of origin underlying the deal will be the focus of talks in вҖӢMexico next week.

item.title: Arxis pops on Nasdaq debut, signals strong demand for aerospace IPOs
item.description: Aerospace and defense parts maker Arxis' shares surged nearly вҖӢ36% in their Nasdaq debut on вҖҢThursday, signaling growing appetite for defense and industrial listings as geopolitical tensions escalate.

item.title: Fed's Miran may scale back rate-cut outlook again due to 'less favorable' inflation developments
item.description: Federal Reserve Governor Stephen Miran, by far the U.S. central bank's most aggressive вҖӢadvocate for fast and immediate interest rate cuts, said on Thursday he вҖҢmay again scale back the pace at which he thinks rates should fall because inflation is proving more persistent than expected.
